title: Understanding the Forces
text: Andrea's force is directed due north, while Jennifer's force is at an angle of $$32$$ degrees east of north. We need to break these forces into their components along the east (i) and north (j) directions.

title: Breaking Down Andrea's Force
text: Since Andrea's force is directed due north, it only has a component in the north direction (j). The magnitude of this force is $$130.0$$ N.

title: Breaking Down Jennifer's Force
text: Jennifer's force can be broken down into two components: one in the east direction (i) and one in the north direction (j). Use trigonometry to find these components.

title: Calculating Jennifer's East Component
text: Use the cosine function to find the east component of Jennifer's force. The formula is: east component $$=$$ magnitude * cos(angle).

title: Calculating Jennifer's North Component
text: Use the sine function to find the north component of Jennifer's force. The formula is: north component $$=$$ magnitude * sin(angle).

title: Combining the Forces
text: To find the combined force, add the components of Andrea's and Jennifer's forces in each direction.

title: Calculating the Combined East Component
text: Add the east components of Andrea's and Jennifer's forces. Andrea's east component is $$0$$ N.

title: Calculating the Combined North Component
text: Add the north components of Andrea's and Jennifer's forces.

title: Finding the Opposing Force
text: To prevent the couch from moving, the combined force exerted by Andrea and Jennifer must be opposed by an equal and opposite force.

title: Calculating the Opposing Force
text: The opposing force should be the negative of the combined force components.
